body.title: Web UI: four-section layout per device tab (health+controls+chat+announce)
body.result: tabbed /rack page with Announce/Health/Controls/Chat sections per device; /api/device/list and /api/device/{id}/events endpoints
body.description:
Per-device tabs in the web UI each show four sections: (1) Announce — scrolling feed of recent channel events for this device (GRANNY_DISPATCH, MINION_RESULT, etc. — machine-to-machine log, most important recent entries); (2) Health/Status — alpha-sorted key=value pairs from the device's health surface, values age out after a configurable TTL and disappear, all cleared on start or when web server asks each device's health; (3) Controls — circuit breakers and action buttons for this device, populated from health surface on startup; (4) Chat — edit box + scrollable chat history for human↔device interaction.

Design: devices generate their own panel from a template/base class in BaseShim or a device health mixin — the web page just renders each device's panel (iframe or div from source). Default format: one long scroll with 4 sub-sections. The status/health/controls pane is the same surface — the health panel IS the control panel for each device.

Status key/value protocol: a device posts messages with key=value pairs to its status channel. The web server collects these, alpha-sorts them, renders live. Any key not updated within TTL (device-configurable) ages out. On web startup, server calls each device's /health endpoint to populate initial state — anything paused/disabled shows as open circuit.

**Affected files:** devices/web_server/server.py; BaseShim or new devices/base/health_mixin.py; each device's shim for health surface implementation; static HTML/JS for four-section layout
**Design rules:** OOP-first — device panel generation lives in BaseShim/mixin, not in server.py; log every status update at DEBUG; no new dependencies
**Scope boundary:** IN: four-section layout; health surface protocol (key/value + TTL); devices generate own panel template; announce feed wired to channel; controls = circuit breakers. OUT: per-device custom CSS, fancy graphics (text + emoji for now), iframe vs div implementation choice (decide at build time).
**Completion criteria:** Opening Granny's tab shows all four sections. Granny posting a GRANNY_DISPATCH causes an entry in the announce pane. A device posting a key=value status message causes it to appear in the health pane and age out after TTL. Existing chat functionality still works. BaseShim has a health_surface() method subclasses can override.
